content.problemStatement:
A study has shown that playing a musical instrument helps in increasing one's IQ by 777 points.
Chef knows he can't beat Einstein in physics, but he wants to try to beat him in an IQ competition.

You know that Einstein had an IQ of 170170170, and Chef currently has an IQ of XXX.

Determine if, after learning to play a musical instrument, Chef's IQ will become **strictly greater than** Einstein's.

Print "Yes" if it is possible for Chef to beat Einstein, else print "No" (without quotes).

You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer XXX, the current IQ of Chef.


Output Format

For each testcase, output in a single line "Yes" or "No"
You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The input consists of one or more lines. Each line contains a single integer X, the current IQ of Chef. (If multiple lines are present, treat each as an independent test case.)
content.outputFormat: For each input line, output "Yes" if Chef's IQ after adding 7 becomes strictly greater than Einstein's IQ (170), otherwise output "No". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 165 + 7 = 172, which is greater than 170, so Chef can beat Einstein.
120 + 7 = 127, which is not greater than 170, so Chef cannot beat Einstein.

hidden.label: edge case equal to Einstein after addition

solution.approach: For each given IQ X, compute X + 7. If the result is greater than 170, Chef can beat Einstein, so print "Yes"; otherwise print "No". The operation is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) where T is the number of test cases (one constant-time check per case).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides input storage.
